skill.name: create-issue
skill.description: Create structured GitHub or Jira issues from a user request, using the repository issue tracker configuration.
skill.summary: Creates structured GitHub or Jira issues with tracker detection, labels, priority, assignee handling, and issue templates.
skill.author: Cloud-Officer

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are true positives because the skill intentionally runs git, gh, and jira commands. These commands match the issue creation purpose, but they can create real tracker records and depend on user-controlled issue text. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are false positives from ordinary template words, with no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Issue Creation Uses External CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to run git, gh, and jira commands. This is expected for an issue creation workflow, but it can create real issues in external services and should run only with user approval and configured credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents concrete shell commands for git, GitHub CLI, and Jira CLI. The commands are legitimate for the stated purpose, but their external side effects are clear.

medium_findings.title: Temporary Issue Body File Is Written and Deleted
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill writes issue content to issue-body.md and deletes it after command execution. This is a normal CLI pattern, but it can leave sensitive issue text on disk if a command fails before cleanup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require writing a temporary issue-body.md file and later deleting it. The residual-file risk is contextual and depends on command success.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptography locations are ordinary issue template text such as description fields, design links, and severity wording. No cryptographic API, hashing routine, encryption operation, or credential handling was found in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain markdown prose and template labels, not code invoking cryptographic primitives. The static pattern appears to match substrings in normal words.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reported reconnaissance lines are template sections about dependencies, blockers, and documentation context. No evidence found of system scanning, port scanning, network probing, or host enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are plain issue template prompts for project planning. They do not instruct network reconnaissance or system discovery commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands With External Side Effects
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses command examples that call gh issue create and jira issue create. These commands can modify external trackers, so marketplace users need a clear warning and should review issue content first.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command names and flags are explicit and create remote tracker issues. The purpose is legitimate, but the side effect is security relevant.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time turning requests into complete tracker issues. This skill drafts structured tickets and creates them in GitHub or Jira using local CLI tools.
actual_capabilities: Detects whether GitHub Issues are enabled for the current repository.
Creates GitHub issues with a title, body file, labels, and optional assignee.
Creates Jira issues with type, priority, label, summary, and optional assignee.
Maps severity levels to Jira priorities for consistent ticket triage.
Uses separate task, bug, and story templates with structured sections.
Deletes the temporary issue body file after the issue creation command runs.
limitations: Requires authenticated and configured gh and jira command line tools.
Depends on the current repository and the user default Jira configuration.
Does not validate that labels, priorities, or assignees exist before creation.
Creates real issues, so users should review generated content before execution.

use_cases.title: Create bug tickets from reports
use_cases.description: Turn reproduction steps, environment details, and impact notes into a structured GitHub or Jira bug issue.

use_cases.title: Capture implementation tasks
use_cases.description: Convert a requested code change into a task with scope, testing notes, and technical context.

use_cases.title: Draft product stories
use_cases.description: Create story issues that include stakeholders, value, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and release notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a straightforward work item.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a task issue for adding a settings link to the account menu. Include scope, testing notes, and no assignee.

prompt_templates.title: Create a bug report
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with severity and reproduction details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a high severity bug issue for checkout failing on Safari. Include environment, steps to reproduce, expected behavior, and workaround.

prompt_templates.title: Create an assigned Jira story
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with ownership and product context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a story assigned to alex for saved payment methods. Include business value, API changes, risks, and measurable acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Create a compliance task
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with security and release planning details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a critical task for rotating exposed staging credentials. Include security impact, mitigation steps, testing, rollback, and monitoring notes.

output_examples.input: Create a bug for users seeing a blank billing page after login.
output_examples.output: A bug issue is created with environment, actual behavior, expected behavior, reproduction steps, severity, workaround, and supporting details.

output_examples.input: Create a Jira story for a new notification preferences page.
output_examples.output: A story issue is created with user groups, business value, design notes, API considerations, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and release notes.

output_examples.input: Create a task to add retry logging to the payment worker.
output_examples.output: A task issue is created with scope, out-of-scope notes, security impact, testing details, and technical implementation context.

best_practices: Review the generated issue body before allowing the creation command to run.
Provide severity, assignee, labels, and tracker preference in the request when known.
Run the skill from the target repository so tracker detection and naming are correct.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts that omit the issue type, impact, or expected outcome.
Do not include secrets, tokens, or private customer data in issue descriptions.
Do not run the skill without authenticated gh or jira CLI configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill create real issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses gh or jira CLI commands to create issues in the configured tracker.

faq.question: How does it choose GitHub or Jira?
faq.answer: It checks whether GitHub Issues are enabled for the current repository, then falls back to Jira when they are disabled.

faq.question: Can it assign issues to a user?
faq.answer: Yes. It adds an assignee when the user request includes an assignee name.

faq.question: Does it support different issue types?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides task, bug, and story templates with different required sections.

faq.question: What tools must be installed?
faq.answer: The repository must have git. GitHub creation requires gh, and Jira creation requires the jira CLI.

faq.question: Does it store ticket content locally?
faq.answer: It writes a temporary issue-body.md file and deletes it after the issue command succeeds.
